name: CBC with Differential
note: Evaluates red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets; screens for anemia, infection, and blood disorders

name: CMP
note: Kidney function, liver enzymes, electrolytes, glucose, and protein; broad metabolic screen

name: BMP
note: Subset of CMP; electrolytes, BUN, creatinine, glucose; often ordered for monitoring

name: Lipid Panel
note: Total cholesterol, LDL, HDL, triglycerides; cardiovascular risk assessment; ideally fasting

name: HbA1c
note: 3-month average blood glucose; diabetes diagnosis and management; optimal <5.4% (functional range)

name: Fasting Glucose
note: Blood sugar after 8+ hour fast; diabetes screening; optimal <90 mg/dL (functional range)

name: Fasting Insulin
note: Best early marker for insulin resistance; optimal <5 mcIU/mL; not routinely ordered but very informative

name: TSH
note: Primary thyroid screening test; functional range 1-2 mIU/L (narrower than lab reference); run with free T3/T4

name: Free T3
note: Active thyroid hormone; often low even when TSH is normal; important for fatigue and metabolic symptoms

name: Free T4
note: Precursor thyroid hormone; converted to T3; low if hypothyroid; often ordered with TSH

name: Reverse T3
note: Inactive T3 form; elevated in chronic stress, illness, and iron deficiency; blocks active T3 receptors

name: TPO Antibodies
note: Primary Hashimoto's thyroiditis marker; positive in autoimmune thyroid disease

name: Thyroglobulin Antibodies
note: Secondary Hashimoto's marker; may be elevated when TPO antibodies are negative

name: Ferritin
note: Iron storage protein; optimal range 50-100 ng/mL for most; low causes fatigue and hair loss

name: Iron Panel
note: Serum iron, TIBC, transferrin saturation; comprehensive iron status assessment beyond ferritin alone

name: Vitamin D 25-OH
note: Serum vitamin D level; optimal range 40-60 ng/mL; frequently deficient; recheck after supplementing

name: Vitamin B12
note: Serum B12; functional deficiency possible even with normal serum levels; check MMA for tissue status

name: Folate
note: RBC folate more accurate than serum; important for methylation and pregnancy screening

name: Homocysteine
note: Methylation biomarker; elevated levels associated with cardiovascular risk; optimal <7 umol/L

name: hsCRP
note: Systemic inflammation marker; optimal <1 mg/L; elevated with infection, autoimmune, and metabolic issues

name: ESR
note: Nonspecific inflammation marker; elevated in autoimmune and chronic inflammatory conditions

name: Uric Acid
note: Gout and insulin resistance marker; elevated with high fructose, alcohol, and metabolic syndrome

name: ALT
note: Primary liver cell damage marker; elevated in NAFLD, hepatitis, and medication toxicity

name: AST
note: Liver enzyme; less liver-specific than ALT; also elevated with muscle damage

name: GGT
note: Sensitive liver and bile duct marker; elevated with alcohol, metabolic syndrome, and oxidative stress

name: Alkaline Phosphatase
note: Liver and bone enzyme; elevated in liver disease, bone disorders, and bile obstruction

name: Bilirubin
note: Liver and red blood cell breakdown marker; elevated in liver disease, bile obstruction, or hemolysis

name: BUN
note: Kidney and protein metabolism marker; elevated in dehydration, high protein intake, or kidney disease

name: Creatinine
note: Kidney filtration marker; used with cystatin C for more accurate GFR estimation

name: eGFR
note: Calculated kidney function; >90 normal; 60-90 mildly reduced; <60 indicates CKD stages

name: Urinalysis
note: Urine color, pH, specific gravity, protein, glucose, ketones, blood, nitrites, leukocytes

name: Urine Culture
note: Identifies specific bacteria causing UTI; guides antibiotic selection; colony count threshold >100,000 CFU/mL

name: Estradiol E2
note: Primary estrogen; varies by cycle phase and menopausal status; run on day 3 or mid-cycle as appropriate

name: Progesterone
note: Run on day 21 of cycle to confirm ovulation; optimal >10 ng/mL mid-luteal for adequate luteal phase

name: Total Testosterone
note: Total testosterone; morning draw preferred; consider free testosterone and SHBG for full picture

name: Free Testosterone
note: Unbound, active testosterone; may be low even with normal total T if SHBG is high

name: DHEA-S
note: Primary adrenal androgen; marker of adrenal reserve; declines with age

name: Cortisol AM Serum
note: Best drawn 8-9am; evaluates adrenal cortisol output; must be interpreted with symptoms and ACTH

name: 4-Point Salivary Cortisol
note: Measures cortisol at 4 time points across the day; maps diurnal rhythm; detects adrenal dysregulation

name: SHBG
note: Protein binding sex hormones; high SHBG reduces free testosterone and estrogen bioavailability

name: LH
note: Triggers ovulation and testosterone production; run day 3 baseline or to confirm LH surge timing

name: FSH
note: Ovarian reserve and pituitary function; day 3 FSH elevated with diminished ovarian reserve; male: sperm

name: Prolactin
note: Elevated in pituitary adenoma, hypothyroidism, and certain medications; affects sex hormones and libido

name: IGF-1
note: Growth hormone surrogate marker; reflects GH secretion; used in longevity and HGH protocols

name: Growth Hormone
note: Random GH less useful than stimulation testing or IGF-1; evaluated in growth disorders and deficiency

name: ACTH Stimulation Test
note: Gold standard for adrenal insufficiency; cortisol response to synthetic ACTH measured; requires clinical setting

name: Aldosterone
note: Mineralocorticoid; evaluated for primary hyperaldosteronism, Conn's syndrome, and salt-wasting

name: Renin
note: Run with aldosterone for aldosterone:renin ratio; screens for primary hyperaldosteronism

name: DUTCH Test
note: Comprehensive dried urine hormone metabolite testing; maps estrogen and androgen metabolism pathways

name: GI-MAP
note: PCR-based stool test for pathogens, parasites, bacteria, and viruses; H. pylori and dysbiosis markers

name: Comprehensive Stool Analysis
note: Microbiome diversity, digestive markers, inflammation (calprotectin), and short-chain fatty acid production

name: Organic Acids Test
note: Urine metabolite test; assesses mitochondrial, methylation, neurotransmitter, and fungal/bacterial markers

name: SIBO Breath Test
note: Lactulose or glucose challenge with hydrogen/methane breath measurement; identifies SIBO location and type

name: Intestinal Permeability Test
note: Evaluates gut barrier integrity; lactulose/mannitol ratio in urine or Cyrex antibody panels

name: Cyrex Array Food Sensitivity
note: Antibody testing for multiple food antigens and cross-reactive proteins; gluten and leaky gut markers

name: Mediator Release Test
note: Functional food sensitivity test measuring mediator release from white blood cells; 170+ foods and chemicals

name: IgG Food Sensitivity Panel
note: Measures IgG antibodies to 90-200 foods; useful as elimination guide though clinical significance debated

name: IgE Allergy Panel
note: Measures true IgE-mediated allergies to foods and environmental allergens; clinical gold standard for allergy

name: Mycotoxin Test Urine
note: Urine mycotoxin panel from mold exposure; ochratoxin, aflatoxin, trichothecenes, and others

name: Heavy Metals Panel Blood
note: Whole blood toxic metals; reflects recent exposure; lead, mercury, cadmium, arsenic common panel

name: Heavy Metals Panel Urine
note: 24-hour or provoked urine toxic metals; reflects total body burden with chelation challenge

name: Mold Antibodies Panel
note: IgE and IgG antibodies to common mold species; supports mold illness and CIRS diagnosis

name: Igenex Lyme Panel
note: More sensitive than standard CDC Lyme testing; Western blot and PCR; includes more bands than standard labs

name: Tick-Borne Coinfection Panel
note: Tests for Babesia, Bartonella, Ehrlichia, Anaplasma, Rocky Mountain spotted fever alongside Lyme

name: RBC Magnesium
note: Better measure of tissue magnesium than serum; serum magnesium is tightly regulated and misleading

name: RBC Zinc
note: More accurate than serum zinc; reflects intracellular zinc stores

name: Plasma Zinc
note: Standard zinc measurement; morning fasting sample; levels vary with time of day and recent meals

name: Whole Blood Selenium
note: Whole blood selenium reflects both recent intake and stored selenium; important for thyroid function

name: Omega-3 Index
note: Measures EPA+DHA as percentage of RBC fatty acids; optimal >8%; cardiovascular risk marker

name: SpectraCell Micronutrient Panel
note: Measures functional intracellular nutrient levels in lymphocytes; comprehensive micronutrient deficiency screen

name: NutrEval
note: Combined plasma amino acids, RBC fatty acids, and urine organic acids; comprehensive nutritional assessment

name: Plasma Amino Acids
note: Quantifies individual amino acid levels in plasma; identifies deficiencies affecting neurotransmitters and metabolism

name: Urine Amino Acids
note: Identifies amino acid wasting in urine; transport defects and metabolic disorders

name: Carnitine Levels
note: Free and acylcarnitine; deficiency causes fatigue and muscle dysfunction; low in vegans and certain conditions

name: CoQ10 Serum
note: Plasma CoQ10 assessment; low with statin use, aging, and mitochondrial dysfunction

name: RBC Glutathione
note: Measures antioxidant capacity; reflects oxidative stress and detoxification capacity

name: Salivary Melatonin
note: Dim light melatonin onset (DLMO) timing; assesses circadian rhythm phase; guides sleep timing recommendations

name: 23andMe Raw Data
note: Raw SNP data; run through Genetic Genie, SelfDecode, or Promethease for methylation and health variants

name: MTHFR Gene Test
note: Tests C677T and A1298C variants; affects folate metabolism and methylation; guides B vitamin supplementation

name: Factor V Leiden
note: Most common inherited thrombophilia; increases DVT/PE risk; important before oral contraceptives or surgery

name: Prothrombin Mutation G20210A
note: Second most common thrombophilia mutation; increases clotting risk; tested alongside Factor V Leiden

name: BRCA1 BRCA2 Testing
note: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer risk assessment; counseling required before and after testing

name: Pharmacogenomics Panel
note: Tests CYP2D6, CYP2C19, and other drug-metabolizing enzyme variants; guides medication dosing

name: Methylation SNP Panel
note: Tests MTHFR, COMT, CBS, MTR, MTRR and other methylation pathway SNPs; guides nutrient protocols

name: DEXA Bone Density Scan
note: Gold standard bone density measurement; T-score and Z-score; screen at menopause or age 65+ (women)

name: DEXA Body Composition
note: Precise body fat percentage, lean mass, and regional fat distribution; superior to BMI

name: Pelvic Ultrasound
note: Evaluates uterus, ovaries, and pelvic organs; fibroids, cysts, endometriosis screening; PCOS follicle count

name: Thyroid Ultrasound
note: Evaluates thyroid nodules, size, and vascularity; recommended with elevated antibodies or palpable nodule

name: Abdominal Ultrasound
note: Evaluates liver, gallbladder, spleen, kidneys, and pancreas; NAFLD screening and gallstone detection

name: Breast Ultrasound
note: Supplements mammography especially for dense breast tissue; characterizes cysts vs. solid masses

name: Mammogram
note: Breast cancer screening; 3D tomosynthesis (DBT) has fewer false positives; screening guidelines vary by organization

name: Colonoscopy
note: Gold standard colorectal cancer screening; baseline at age 45; polyp removal during procedure

name: Upper Endoscopy
note: Evaluates esophagus, stomach, and duodenum; GERD, H. pylori biopsy, celiac diagnosis, dysphagia

name: Echocardiogram
note: Cardiac structure and function imaging; ejection fraction, valves, and wall motion assessment

name: EKG
note: Electrical heart activity; arrhythmia detection, cardiac screening, medication monitoring

name: Stress Test
note: Exercise EKG for coronary artery disease evaluation; stress echo or nuclear imaging adds imaging

name: Coronary Calcium Score
note: CT measurement of coronary artery calcification; powerful cardiovascular risk predictor; Agatston score

name: ApoB
note: More accurate cardiovascular risk marker than LDL-C; counts all atherogenic particles; target <80 mg/dL optimal

name: ApoA1
note: HDL functional protein; ApoB:ApoA1 ratio is potent cardiovascular risk predictor

name: Oxidized LDL
note: Measures atherogenic oxidized LDL particles; more predictive of plaque than standard LDL-C

name: Lipoprotein(a)
note: Genetically determined cardiovascular risk factor; elevated in 20% of population; test once in lifetime

name: NMR LipoProfile
note: Nuclear magnetic resonance particle size and number analysis; LDL-P number more predictive than LDL-C

name: Continuous Glucose Monitor
note: Real-time interstitial glucose measurement; reveals post-meal spikes and metabolic patterns; 10-14 day wear

name: Blood Pressure Home Monitoring
note: Daily home blood pressure monitoring; more accurate than white coat readings; optimal <120/80

name: HRV Tracking
note: Autonomic nervous system balance marker; higher HRV = better resilience; tracked with wearables

name: Sleep Tracking
note: Sleep staging, duration, and quality monitoring; guides sleep hygiene optimization; Oura and WHOOP most common

name: Basal Body Temperature Tracking
note: Confirms ovulation via post-ovulatory temperature rise; used in fertility awareness and thyroid assessment

name: Peak Flow Meter
note: Home asthma monitoring; measures peak expiratory flow; guides medication adjustments

name: SpO2 Monitoring
note: Non-invasive blood oxygen saturation; useful for respiratory illness, sleep apnea screening, and altitude

name: Resting Heart Rate Tracking
note: Elevated resting HR indicates overtraining, illness, or stress; tracked passively with wearables

name: Body Temperature Wearable
note: Continuous temperature tracking for illness detection, ovulation, and circadian rhythm assessment

name: CIMT
note: Measures arterial wall thickness as surrogate for atherosclerosis; predicts cardiovascular age

name: VO2 Max Test
note: Gold standard cardiorespiratory fitness test; strong mortality predictor; lab treadmill or submaximal estimate

name: Grip Strength Test
note: Simple but powerful longevity predictor; measured with dynamometer; low grip predicts adverse outcomes

name: Balance Testing
note: Balance markers correlate with falls risk and neurological health; simple single-leg stand test

name: GlycanAge Test
note: Measures IgG glycosylation patterns as biological age proxy; inflammation and immune aging marker

name: TruAge Methylation Age
note: Epigenetic aging clock using DNA methylation; Horvath or Levine clock variants; TruDiagnostic test

name: CLIA Microbiome Analysis
note: Comprehensive gut microbiome sequencing; diversity, keystone species, and pathway analysis

name: Oral Microbiome Test
note: Identifies oral pathogens linked to systemic inflammation and cardiovascular disease

name: Lactate Testing
note: Exercise intensity zone assessment; identifies lactate threshold for training optimization

name: Anti-nuclear Antibody
note: Autoimmune screening test; positive in lupus, Sjogren's, and other autoimmune conditions; reflex to titer

name: Complement C3 C4
note: Innate immune complement proteins; low in active lupus and immune complex disease; used in CIRS

name: TGF-beta 1
note: Pro-fibrotic cytokine; elevated in mold illness (CIRS) and tissue remodeling conditions

name: MMP-9
note: Tissue remodeling enzyme; elevated in CIRS, Lyme, and inflammatory conditions

name: Vasoactive Intestinal Polypeptide
note: Regulatory neuropeptide; low in advanced CIRS; VIP nasal spray used therapeutically

name: MSH
note: Regulatory peptide low in mold illness and CIRS; affects sleep, pain, and mucosal immunity

name: VEGF
note: Low in CIRS and mold illness; high in some cancers; regulates blood vessel formation

name: Antigliadin Antibodies
note: Antibodies to gliadin protein; useful for gluten sensitivity even without celiac disease

name: Endomysial Antibodies
note: Highly specific for celiac disease; IgA EMA; needs IgA sufficiency for accurate interpretation

name: tTG-IgA
note: Primary celiac disease screening test; must be on gluten-containing diet for accurate results

name: Comprehensive Hormone Panel
note: Broad hormonal assessment including thyroid, sex hormones, adrenal markers, and metabolic hormones

name: Stool Calprotectin
note: Sensitive marker for gut inflammation; elevated in IBD; helps distinguish IBD from IBS

name: Fecal Zonulin
note: Marker of tight junction proteins; elevated in leaky gut and gluten exposure
